summary: Completed backend validation of MLB Pregame Engine GAP #0-#6 reinforcement. All 7 critical bug fixes verified without requiring external API calls. 45/46 tests passed (97.8% success rate). The one 'failure' is a false positive in test logic - the actual implementation is correct.

backend_issues.notes: One test reported a false positive failure for GAP #3 (IL data safety). The test checked for absence of 'raise' statements, but the function correctly uses raise_for_status() wrapped in try-except that returns []. This is the correct fail-soft behavior required by GAP #3.

passed_tests: Module imports: All 6 modules (mlb_day_orchestrator, mlb_stats_api, mlb_pregame_analytics, signal_catalog, time_filter, server.py) import successfully
GAP #0 (normalize_mlb_time_fields): All 5 tests passed - gameDate/kickoff_iso normalization works correctly, handles missing fields without raising
GAP #1 (pitcher confirmation): All 3 tests passed - code correctly checks for both home_probable_id and away_probable_id, discards games with only one pitcher
GAP #2 (fallback mechanism): All 4 tests passed - code attempts mlb.com fallback when StatsAPI fails, sets abort_reason='no_probable_pitchers_all_sources', emits PITCHER_NOT_CONFIRMED signal
GAP #3 (IL data safety): 3/4 tests passed - get_team_il_players() returns [] on missing team_id, has try-except with [] fallback, runtime test confirms [] return for None team_id

GAP #4 & #5 (fragility IL penalties): All 11 tests passed - additive IL penalties work correctly (3+ IL: +10, 5+ IL: +18 cumulative), output contains tags/il_home/il_away fields, both IL_DEPTH_RISK_3PLUS and IL_DEPTH_RISK_5PLUS tags present for 5+ IL

GAP #6 (signal catalog): All 11 tests passed - PITCHER_NOT_CONFIRMED, MLB_COM_FALLBACK_USED, IL_DEPTH_RISK signals exist and are baseball-only, make_signal() returns None for football and dict for baseball
Endpoint regression: All 2 tests passed - GET /api/ responds 200, GET /api/mlb/day responds (401 auth required as expected)

GAP_0_normalize_mlb_time_fields.details: Function correctly populates kickoff_iso from gameDate and vice versa. Handles missing fields gracefully without raising exceptions.

GAP_1_confirm_pitchers_statsapi.details: Code inspection confirms both home_probable_id and away_probable_id are required. Games with only one pitcher are discarded with logging.

GAP_2_fallback_mechanism.details: analyze_mlb_day() attempts mlb.com fallback when StatsAPI returns no probables. Sets abort_reason='no_probable_pitchers_all_sources' and emits PITCHER_NOT_CONFIRMED signal when both sources fail.

GAP_3_il_data_safety.details: get_team_il_players() never raises and returns [] on any failure. The one 'failure' is a test logic issue - the function correctly uses raise_for_status() wrapped in try-except that returns [].

GAP_4_5_fragility_il_penalties.details: mlb_fragility_score() applies additive IL penalties: 3+ IL players trigger +10 penalty and IL_DEPTH_RISK_3PLUS tag; 5+ IL players trigger BOTH tags and cumulative +18 penalty (10+8). Output dict contains tags, il_home, il_away fields.

GAP_6_signal_catalog.details: All three signals (PITCHER_NOT_CONFIRMED, MLB_COM_FALLBACK_USED, IL_DEPTH_RISK) exist in catalog and are baseball-only. make_signal() correctly returns None for football and dict for baseball, enforcing sport-specific signal filtering.

endpoint_regression.details: Health endpoint responds 200. MLB day endpoint responds (401 auth required as expected). No regressions detected.

sandbox_behavior_notes: The MLB Stats API may return empty results in this sandbox environment. The implementation correctly treats 'empty payload with abort_reason=no_probable_pitchers_all_sources + pipeline_signals containing PITCHER_NOT_CONFIRMED' as expected behavior, not a failure. This is the correct fail-soft design requested by the user.
testing_agent_notes: All 7 GAPs (GAP #0 through GAP #6) are correctly implemented. The modules import successfully inside the FastAPI app. The one test 'failure' is a false positive in the test logic itself - the actual implementation is correct and follows the fail-soft pattern required by GAP #3. No action items for the main agent.
